summary: Iteration 28 - Discovered Endpoints Feature + 10G Bug Fix. All backend (18/18) and frontend tests passed. Topology now shows 20 nodes (9 managed + 11 discovered endpoints). Bug fix confirmed: inferred edges to .6 and .7 no longer have '10G Uplink' labels - only MAC table edges have 10G labels.

backend_endpoint_attributes: All endpoints have: role=discovered_endpoint, mac, ip, switch_ip, switch_port, layer=5
Endpoint types correctly inferred: server, printer, camera, ap, nas, generic
backend_10g_bug_fix: Edges to .6 have type='access' with empty label (no 10G)
Edges to .7 have type='access' with empty label (no 10G)
No inferred edges have '10G' in label
Only MAC table edges have 10G labels
backend_mac_table_edges: MAC edge to .4: 'Port 9 (10G)' with type=trunk, source=mac_table
MAC edge to .5: 'Port 10 (10G)' with type=trunk, source=mac_table
backend_mac_discovery_edges: All edges from switch to endpoint have port number in label
frontend_network_map: Navigate to Stato Rete -> Mappa tab works
11 MAC addresses displayed on endpoint nodes
frontend_edge_labels: Edge labels include 'Port 9 (10G)' and 'Port 10 (10G)' for MAC table edges
Edge labels include '1G' for access edges
Edge labels include 'Port X' for mac_discovery edges
frontend_legend: Legend shows: WAN (cyan), 10G (orange), Trunk (purple), Accesso (1G) (gray), Server (green), MGMT (amber), LLDP (cyan), Manuale (purple)

critical_code_review_comments: connector.py correctly saves ALL MAC entries to discovered_endpoints collection (not just managed devices)
topology.py _guess_endpoint_type function correctly classifies endpoints by hostname patterns
topology.py adds discovered_endpoints as layer 5 nodes with role=discovered_endpoint
Bug fix: inferred edges no longer have '10G Uplink' labels - only MAC table edges with confirmed 10G port speeds get 10G labels
NetworkMap.js correctly displays MAC address, port, and VLAN on endpoint nodes
Edge styling: 10G edges are orange (#f97316) and thick (strokeWidth=3), access edges are gray
seed_data_creation: Used existing seed data with 8 managed devices + 11 discovered_endpoints in test_database
context_for_next_testing_agent: Discovered endpoints feature fully validated. Backend: GET /api/network/topology/{client_id} returns 20 nodes (9 managed + 11 discovered endpoints at layer 5). Each endpoint has: role=discovered_endpoint, mac, ip, switch_ip, switch_port, layer=5. Bug fix confirmed: edges to .6 and .7 have type='access' with no 10G label. Only MAC table edges (to .4 and .5) have 10G labels. Frontend: React Flow map shows all 20 nodes with MAC addresses visible on endpoints, port labels on edges, and correct legend with 10G in orange.

mac_discovery: 11 edges from switches to discovered endpoints with Port X labels

topology_validation.bug_fix_verified: Edges to .6 and .7 no longer have '10G Uplink' label - only MAC table edges can have 10G
